description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the telegram connector arranged on the setup spectrum so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's true capability profile. Telegram bots cannot create chats or cold-DM a user, so most scenarios act on an operator-provisioned sandbox chat the bot already belongs to (arrange-act builds a message inside it; discover-act resolves a chat via recent updates). Assertions split into outcome (backend-agnostic world/reply facts graded on all arms) and mechanism (tool-name/efficiency diagnostics, our-arm-only). {{run_id}} namespaces created content. Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

fixtures.name: telegram_sandbox_chat
fixtures.description: A Telegram group or supergroup the eval bot belongs to (ideally as an admin, so it can pin and read administrators). A bot cannot create a chat or message a user first, so a human must add the bot to the group; set EVAL_TELEGRAM_SANDBOX_CHAT to that chat's id or @username.
fixtures.expects: A Telegram group or supergroup that the eval bot is a member of and can send, edit, delete, and pin its own messages in, and read membership of. Identified by the resolved value of this fixture as a chat_id — a numeric id (supergroups are -100-prefixed, e.g. -1001234567890) or a public @username. For pinning to work the bot must have the can_pin_messages admin right; for administrator/member reads the chat must be a group/supergroup (a private chat has no administrators). To verify: call getChat with the id and confirm it resolves to a group/supergroup the bot can see; getMe confirms the bot identity; optionally send then delete a probe message to confirm write access. Because a bot cannot start a chat, this fixture can only be provisioned by a human adding the bot to the group.

evals.description: Single-capability probe: post a plain text message to a named ambient chat.

steps.intent: Send the message 'Standup reminder {{run_id}}' to the chat {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}.

outcome: A message with the text 'Standup reminder {{run_id}}' is delivered to the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat and its message id is reported.
mechanism: Sends to the chat id given in the request with a single send-message call; does not attempt to create a chat or invent a different chat id.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: post an update with a bold heading and a link; the naive standard-Markdown path renders as literal markup, the correct path renders formatting.

steps.intent: Post this update to {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}: a bold heading reading 'Release {{run_id}}', then a new line with a link labeled 'changelog' pointing to https://example.com/changelog.

outcome: The message delivered to {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} shows 'Release {{run_id}}' as bold text and 'changelog' as a clickable link — not as literal markup characters (e.g. not a literal '**Release**' or '[changelog](...)').
mechanism: Sends with parse_mode set to HTML (or MarkdownV2 with the reserved characters properly escaped), rather than passing raw standard Markdown as plain text.

evals.description: Single-capability probe: send a photo from a public image URL with a caption.

steps.intent: Send the photo at https://example.com/banner.jpg to {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} with the caption 'Launch banner {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A photo message with the caption 'Launch banner {{run_id}}' is delivered to the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat.
mechanism: Passes the image URL (or a Telegram file_id) as the photo in a single send-photo call; does not attempt to read or upload a local file.

evals.description: Single-capability probe: create a poll with a question and several options.

steps.intent: Create a poll in {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} asking 'Lunch spot {{run_id}}?' with the options Tacos, Sushi, and Salad.

outcome: A poll asking 'Lunch spot {{run_id}}?' with the three options Tacos, Sushi, and Salad now exists in the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat.
mechanism: Sends the three options as a list/array of strings to a single send-poll call; does not concatenate them into one option.

evals.description: Single-capability probe: share a point on the map.

steps.intent: Share the location of the Eiffel Tower (latitude 48.8584, longitude 2.2945) in {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}.

outcome: A location at approximately latitude 48.8584, longitude 2.2945 is shared in the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat.
mechanism: Passes the latitude and longitude as numeric coordinates to a single send-location call.

evals.description: Coverage probe: post a message, then edit its text — one chained session.

steps.intent: Send the message 'Deploy starting {{run_id}}' to {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}.

outcome: A message reading 'Deploy starting {{run_id}}' is delivered to the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat and its message id is reported.
mechanism: Uses a single send-message call; the message id comes from the send result.

steps.intent: Update that message to read 'Deploy complete {{run_id}}' instead.

outcome: A message in the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat now reads 'Deploy complete {{run_id}}'.
mechanism: Edits the existing message by its message id (an edit-message call), rather than sending a new message; does not fabricate a message id.

evals.description: Coverage probe: post a message, then delete it — one chained session.

steps.intent: Send the message 'Oops wrong channel {{run_id}}' to {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}.

outcome: A message reading 'Oops wrong channel {{run_id}}' is delivered to the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat and its message id is reported.
mechanism: Uses a single send-message call; the message id comes from the send result.

steps.intent: Delete the message you just sent.

outcome: The message is removed from the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat (deletion is confirmed).
mechanism: Calls delete-message with the concrete message id from the send earlier in this session; does not delete an unrelated message or fabricate an id.

evals.description: Coverage probe: post a message, pin it, then unpin it — one chained session.

steps.intent: Send the message 'Read me first {{run_id}}' to {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}.

outcome: A message reading 'Read me first {{run_id}}' is delivered to the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat and its message id is reported.
mechanism: Uses a single send-message call; the message id comes from the send result.

steps.intent: Pin that message in the chat.

outcome: The message is pinned in the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat (the pin is confirmed).
mechanism: Calls pin-chat-message with the message id from the send earlier in this session.

steps.intent: Now unpin it.

outcome: The message is no longer pinned in the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat.
mechanism: Calls unpin-chat-message targeting the same message rather than fabricating a different id.

evals.description: Coverage probe: post a message, then forward it (keeping attribution) — one chained session.

steps.intent: Send the message 'Quarterly numbers {{run_id}}' to {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}.

outcome: A message reading 'Quarterly numbers {{run_id}}' is delivered to the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat and its message id is reported.
mechanism: Uses a single send-message call; the message id comes from the send result.

steps.intent: Forward that message again into {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}.

outcome: A forwarded copy of the 'Quarterly numbers {{run_id}}' message appears in the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat.
mechanism: Uses a forward-message call referencing the source chat and the message id, rather than composing a brand-new message with the same text.

evals.description: Single read with no fixture: confirm which bot the connection controls / that the connection works.

steps.intent: Which Telegram bot does this connection control? Confirm the connection is working.

outcome: The reply identifies the bot (its username and/or display name) and indicates the connection/token is valid.
mechanism: Resolves the bot identity from a get-me call rather than asking the user or guessing a name.

evals.description: Head-to-head: discover a chat the bot has recently interacted with, then post to it — exercises chat_id resolution.

steps.intent: List the chats this bot has recently interacted with.

outcome: The reply lists the chats the bot has recently interacted with (their ids/types/names), or clearly states there are none if the recent-updates feed is empty.
mechanism: Reads the recent chats from the connector rather than inventing chat ids or asking the user to supply one.

steps.intent: Send 'Checking in {{run_id}}' to one of those recent chats.

outcome: A message 'Checking in {{run_id}}' is delivered to one of the chats from the recent-chats list (or, if there were none, the agent says so rather than messaging a fabricated chat).
mechanism: Uses a chat id taken from the recent-chats result, not a hard-coded or invented id.

evals.description: Single read: fetch details about a named chat.

steps.intent: Get the details of the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat — its type, title, and id.

outcome: The reply reports the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat's type, title, and id.
mechanism: Resolves the chat with a single get-chat call using the id/username given in the request.

evals.description: Head-to-head: report a chat's member count and who its administrators are.

steps.intent: How many members are in {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}?

outcome: The reply reports a member count for the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat.
mechanism: Reads the count from a member-count call on the chat id given in the request.

steps.intent: Who are the administrators of that chat?

outcome: The reply lists the administrators of the {{telegram_sandbox_chat}} chat (their names and/or usernames).
mechanism: Reads the administrators from a get-administrators call on the same chat; does not fabricate users.

evals.description: No-op/ask probe: an underspecified target should make the agent ask which chat, not silently pick one.

steps.intent: Send 'Heads up {{run_id}}' to my team chat.

outcome: When the bot has more than one chat that could match 'my team chat', the reply asks the user which chat to use (or lists candidates) rather than silently picking one and sending.
mechanism: Does not send any message before the target chat is unambiguously identified.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: a Telegram bot cannot create a group/chat; the agent should say so rather than fabricate success.

steps.intent: Create a new Telegram group called 'Eval Team {{run_id}}' and add my teammates.

outcome: The reply states that creating a Telegram group/chat is not something this connector (or a bot) can do, and does not claim a group was created.
mechanism: Does not call any tool that fabricates a group-creation result.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: member moderation (banning) is not in this connector's surface; the agent should decline cleanly. Roadmap signal for the deferred admin group.

steps.intent: Ban the user @spammer123 from {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}.

outcome: The reply states that banning or removing members is not supported by this connector, and does not claim the user was banned.
mechanism: Does not call any mutating tool in an attempt to ban or restrict the member.

evals.description: Graceful refusal / reframe: the connector sends files by URL or file_id, not from local disk; the agent should ask for a URL rather than fabricate an upload.

steps.intent: Upload the file at /Users/me/report.pdf to {{telegram_sandbox_chat}}.

outcome: The reply explains it can send a file by public URL or an existing Telegram file_id but cannot read a local-disk path, and asks for a URL (or file_id) — it does not claim the local file was uploaded.
mechanism: Does not call a send tool with the local filesystem path as if it were a URL or file_id.
